mnemonics.title: Mapping Order
mnemonics.content: Every Weak Entity Needs Regular Parents First - E-R-W-P: Entities → Relationships → Weak entities → Specialization/Generalization

mnemonics.title: Relationship Mapping
mnemonics.content: 1 to 1: Choose One, 1 to N: Add to Many, M to N: Create New - '1M1' rule: put '1' key in 'M' side, create new for 'M to N'

acronyms.expansion: Primary Key - Unique identifier for a relation
acronyms.usage: Selected from entity identifiers during mapping

acronyms.expansion: Foreign Key - Reference to primary key of another relation
acronyms.usage: Represents relationships between entities in relational schema

acronyms.expansion: Entity-Relationship Diagram - Conceptual view of database

acronyms.usage: Used for partial participation in foreign keys

visualTips.visualization: Think of a tree structure - one trunk (1) connects to many branches (N). The branch carries reference to its trunk.
Think of a many-to-many connection like students and courses - need a bridge/crosswalk (enrollment table) to connect them.
Think of a child holding hands with parent - cannot stand alone, needs parent's support (primary key) to be identified.
Think of inheritance - like a Vehicle class with Car and Bike subclasses sharing common Vehicle attributes.
Think of phone numbers for a person - one person can have multiple phones, so create separate 'Phone' table with person ID.

analogies.analogy: Like translating a storyboard (ER) into a novel (Relational). The storyboard shows characters, their traits, and connections. The novel arranges everything into chapters and sentences (tables and rows).
Like a dependent on an insurance policy - they don't have their own identity, identified by their relationship to the policyholder (owner).
Like a concert ticket having both date and seat number - either alone is not unique, but together they uniquely identify a specific seat for a specific show.
Like being in a mandatory class - you MUST be assigned to a class (cannot be NULL). Partial participation is like an optional club - you may or may not join (can be NULL).
